steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage’s decisive clue is the remark that Cherokee people are not known to have applied genre divisions to their stories before Mooney’s work.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D — no reason to believe that the Cherokee elders who provided the stories would have agreed with Mooney that the inclusion of geographical specificity in some stories marked those stories as different in kind from other stories. follows directly: it says there is no reason to believe the elders would have agreed with Mooney that geographic specificity marked those stories as a different kind.

The other three options either ask about the accuracy of the geographic details, speculate about other untold stories, or wonder about Long’s influence—none of which are supported by the clue about Cherokee genre concepts.

That matches Choice D — no reason to believe that the Cherokee elders who provided the stories would have agreed with Mooney that the inclusion of geographical specificity in some stories marked those stories as different in kind from other stories..
